section_miscategorizations.text: The patient's blood sugar has been running at 100 and 120.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a numeric physiologic value that reads as an objective measurement rather than a subjective symptom description; it would be clearer in Findings, though its presence in HPI is not seriously misleading.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient's blood sugar has been running at 160 over 87.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This appears to be a blood pressure value mislabeled as blood sugar; as an objective vital sign it belongs in Findings, and its current placement among subjective history plus the mislabeling could confuse interpretation of the clinical picture.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient's blood pressure reading was 175 over 92.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is an objective blood pressure measurement and fits better under Findings; however, including a specific recent BP in the HPI as part of the narrative of 'running high' is common and not highly misleading.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports that their blood pressure has been running high.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This summarizes an ongoing problem (hypertension) rather than a discrete symptom; it could be more appropriately synthesized in the Assessment, though as a patient-reported concern it is acceptable in HPI and not seriously misleading.
